---
title: "Session 1: Getting Started with AI Tools"
subtitle: "April 22, 2026 — AI Bootcamp for UVM Economics"
---

## Overview

**Time**: 1.5 hours (lunch session)
**Goal**: Get faculty comfortable using AI tools for everyday academic tasks. By the end, everyone should have used an AI tool to produce something useful.

### Slides

| Deck | Format |
|------|--------|
| Getting Started (Beam & Aslim) | [HTML](slides/s1-intro.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/s1-intro.pdf) |
| Terminal + Claude Code (Beam) | [HTML](slides/s1-emily.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/s1-emily.pdf) |
| Desktop AI + Teaching Applications (Aslim) | [HTML](slides/s1-erkmen.html){target="_blank"} / [PDF](slides/s1-erkmen.pdf) |

## The AI Ladder

Where are you today?

| Level | Description | Example |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| 0 | Haven't tried it | — |
| 1 | Tried it once, wasn't impressed | Asked ChatGPT a question, got a bad answer |
| 2 | Use it occasionally for simple tasks | Drafting emails, brainstorming |
| 3 | Regular user, integrated into workflow | Writing assistance, code help, literature search |
| 4 | Power user, multi-step workflows | Research pipelines, automated grading, custom tools |
| 5 | Building systems with AI | Custom agents, teaching tools, research infrastructure |

**Our goal today**: Get everyone to at least Level 2, and show you what Levels 3–4 look like.

::: {.callout-note}
## Format
This is a watch-along session — we'll demo everything live. No installation required. Slides will be up during generation/loading time so you can follow the concepts while the AI works.
:::

## Part 1: Terminal + Claude Code (Emily) {#terminal-demo}

*~30 minutes*

### Why the terminal?

The desktop chat interfaces (ChatGPT, Claude.ai) are great for quick questions. But the terminal unlocks:

- **File access**: AI can read and write files on your computer
- **Iteration**: Work on a document across multiple steps without copy-pasting
- **Reproducibility**: Save your workflow, rerun it later
- **Power tools**: Claude Code can search your files, run code, and build things

### What you're seeing

1. **Ghostty** — a terminal app (any terminal works; this one just looks nice)
2. **Claude Code** — an AI assistant that runs in the terminal. Type `claude` to launch.
3. **Live prompting** — we type a request, the AI responds, we iterate.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Things you could try later on your own
- "Read my syllabus at [path] and list all the due dates in a table"
- "I have a CSV of student grades. Summarize the distribution and flag any outliers."
- "Draft a response to this student email: [paste email]"
:::

### Key concepts

- **Context window**: The AI can only "see" what you show it. Be explicit about what you want.
- **Iteration**: Your first output won't be perfect. Refine: "Make it shorter," "Add a section on X," "Change the tone to be more formal."
- **Markdown output**: Claude Code writes in Markdown. Use [Typora](https://typora.io) to view it nicely.

### Live demo: terminal basics → Claude Code → iterating on output

<!-- DEMO SCRIPT (Emily):
1. Open Ghostty, show the terminal briefly
2. Type `claude` to launch Claude Code
3. Ask it to read a sample syllabus and extract due dates
4. Ask it to reformat the dates as a calendar-ready CSV
5. Show the output in Typora
6. Show how to start a new conversation (clear context)
-->

## Part 2: Desktop App Demo (Erkmen) {#desktop-demo}

*~15 minutes*

<!-- Erkmen: demo of ChatGPT or Claude desktop for quick tasks -->
<!-- Suggested flow: show the chat interface, paste in a task, show how it differs from terminal -->

## Part 3: Teaching Application (Erkmen) {#teaching-app}

*~25 minutes*

### Live demo: Assignment creation + grading rubric

<!-- Erkmen: build an assignment prompt and grading rubric live
Suggested flow:
1. Start with a course topic
2. Ask AI to generate an assignment prompt
3. Iterate on it (adjust difficulty, add constraints)
4. Generate a grading rubric
5. Show how the rubric could be used for consistent grading
-->

## Wrap-up + Q&A

*~5 minutes*

### Before next session (April 27)

- [ ] Try using Claude Code or a desktop AI tool for one real task this week
- [ ] Install the tools from the [prep guide](prep.qmd) if you haven't already
- [ ] Write down one research or teaching task you'd like to automate

### Resources

- [Claude Code documentation](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code)
- [Anthropic prompt engineering guide](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview)
- [UVM Microsoft Copilot](https://go.uvm.edu/copilot) (free for all UVM)
